SEARCH
Package it.polimi.ingsfw.ingsfwproject.Controller

Class GameController

java.lang.Object
it.polimi.ingsfw.ingsfwproject.Controller.GameController
All Implemented Interfaces:
Controller
public class GameController extends Object implements Controller
The GameController class implements the Controller interface to manage game-related logic and interactions. This controller is responsible for coordinating actions and behaviors within the game, handling player input, updating the game state, and interacting with the game model and view components.

    • globalChat

      public Queue<ChatMessage> globalChat

  • Constructor Details

    • GameController

      public GameController(Game model, GameServerInstance serverInstance)
      Constructs a new GameController.
      Parameters:
      model - the Game that this controller is managing.
      serverInstance - the GameServerInstance that manages.

  • Method Details

    • chooseObjectiveCard

      public void chooseObjectiveCard(String username, int cardID)
      This method allows a player to choose an ObjectiveCard during the initial phase of the game. It sends an ExceptionMessage if it's not the turn of the player that called this method, or if the Gamestate is not equal to CHOOSING_OBJECTIVE.
      Parameters:
      username - the username of the player that want to choose a ObjectiveCard.
      cardID - the Card ID of the card that the player wants to choose.

    • playCard

      public void playCard(String username, int cardID, boolean upwards, Coordinate coord)
      Allows a player to play a card in their Playerground. This method performs several checks before allowing the action:
      • Sends an error message to the client if it's not the player's turn.
      • Sends an error message to the client if the player doesn't have available positions.
      • Sends an error message to the client if the GameState is one of the following: WAITING_FOR_PLAYERS, CHOOSING_OBJECTIVES, ENDED, or CHOOSING_COLOR.
      • Sends an error message to the client if the player has already played a card this turn.
      • Sends an error message to the client if the chosen Coordinate is not available.
      If all checks pass, the method allows the player to play the card and then enables them to draw/pick a card.
      Parameters:
      username - the username of the player who wants to play a card.
      cardID - the ID of the card the player wants to play.
      upwards - true if the player wants to play the card upwards, false if downwards.
      coord - the Coordinate where the player wants to play the card.

    • drawDisplayedPlayableCard

      public void drawDisplayedPlayableCard(String username, int cardID)
      Allows a player to draw one of the displayed card.
      Parameters:
      username - the player who wants to draw a displayed card
      cardID - the id of the card that he wants to draw.

    • draw

      public void draw(String username, boolean resourceDeck)
      Allows a player to draw from a deck.
      Parameters:
      username - the player who wants to draw from a deck.
      resourceDeck - true to draw from ResourceDeck, false to draw from GoldDeck.

    • chooseColor

      public void chooseColor(String username, PlayerColor color)
      Allows a player to choose a color. It sends an ExceptionMessage if it's not the player's turn or if the GameState isn't equal to CHOOSING_COLOR.
      Parameters:
      username - the player who wants to choose a color.
      color - the color that the player wants to pick.

    • addMessageToGlobalChat

      public void addMessageToGlobalChat(ChatMessage message)
      This method sends the global message passed as a parameter to all players in the game.
      Parameters:
      message - the ChatMessage that needs to be sent.

    • forwardPrivateChatMessage

      public void forwardPrivateChatMessage(ChatMessage message)
      This method sends the message passed as a parameter to the recipient contained in the message.
      Parameters:
      message - the ChatMessage that needs to be sent.

    • getModel

      public Game getModel()
      Returns the Game to which this controller is connected.
      Returns:
      the Game to which this controller is connected.

    • getPlayer

      public Player getPlayer(String username)
      Retrieves the player object associated with the given username.
      Parameters:
      username - the username of the player to retrieve.
      Returns:
      the Player object associated with the given username.
